expectations.text: Output includes standard .kql comment header block
expectations.evidence: File opens with '// ===...===' block containing Title, Service: Sentinel, Tables: DeviceLogonEvents, IdentityLogonEvents, Description, Parameters (lookbackWindow, rollingWindow, machineThreshold), Complexity: Advanced
Uses 'DeviceLogonEvents' — listed as an acceptable table in the assertion

expectations.text: Groups by 1-hour time bins (bin(Timestamp, 1h) or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Uses a self-join approach with rolling window: 'where Timestamp >= WindowStart and Timestamp < WindowStart + rollingWindow' where rollingWindow = 1h. Deduplication uses 'bin(WindowStart, rollingWindow)'. This is a sliding-window approach equivalent to 1h grouping.

expectations.text: Counts distinct devices/machines per account per time window
expectations.evidence: 'DistinctMachines = dcount(DeviceName)' in the summarize step, grouped by AccountName, AccountDomain, WindowStart

expectations.text: Filters for accounts hitting more than 5 distinct devices
Covers the last 48 hours as specified

claims.claim: Uses sliding window join (not fixed bin) to avoid missing attacks that span bin boundaries
claims.evidence: Self-join approach with 'where Timestamp >= WindowStart and Timestamp < WindowStart + rollingWindow' is a true sliding window, not fixed bins

claims.claim: Deduplicates overlapping windows per account using arg_max
claims.evidence: Second summarize uses 'arg_max(DistinctMachines, *)' grouped by AccountName, AccountDomain, bin(WindowStart, rollingWindow)

claims.claim: Excludes machine accounts (ending in $) and well-known service accounts

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions pass with strong evidence. The query exceeds requirements with sliding window approach and deduplication logic.
